title: Callosmia fragrans
intro: Callospermum fragrans, also known as Callosmia fragrans, is a plant species within the orchid family, characterized by its unique fragrance and medicinal properties. The health benefits of Callosmia fragrans include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory health, and exhibit antimicrobial properties, making it a valuable herbal remedy for various ailments. The bioactive constituents of Callosmia fragrans include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. The most common herbal preparations made with Callosmia fragrans include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as to support immune function.
taxonomy: Callosmia fragrans is a species of orchid, which falls under the family Orchidaceae. This family is further divided into several subfamilies, with Callosmia fragrans belonging to the subfamily Epidendroideae. At the order level, Callosmia fragrans is classified under Asparagales, a large and diverse order of flowering plants. The class and division levels categorize Callosmia fragrans as a monocot and a flowering plant, respectively. Furthermore, Callosmia fragrans is also classified under the kingdom Plantae.
